query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "mega-asr: towards in-the-wild^2 speech recognition via scaling up real" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the core mechanism of integrating external Language Models into end-to-end Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) systems, which is the central technical challenge of the user's "Mega-ASR" scaling and in-the-wild speech recognition question. Even though it proposes a specific "Density Ratio" method rather than the user's specific scaling approach, it provides the canonical empirical baseline and alternative methodology for the same domain
